name: Santa Pola Municipal Loop Trail: Stage 2
name_original: Parque Natural de las Salinas de Santa Pola — Circular desde Santa Pola
description: Hike the difficult 17.6-mile Santa Pola Municipal Loop Trail: Stage 2 through salt marshes, spotting flamingos and enjoying flat terrain.
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Pola offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by its coastal position on the Costa Blanca. The region features the Sierra de Santa Pola, a fossil reef mountain range with moderate hills, alongside extensive flat salt marshes and protected nature reserves like Clot de Galvany. Coastal paths and promenades provide routes with sea views, while areas like Barranc de Salt offer wilder ravine environments. Best hiking trails around Santa Pola The most popular hiking route is Sierra de Santa Pola (Circular PR-CV 61), a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route offers views of Santa Pola and Alicante, passing historical remnants. Another top favourite among local hikers is Parque de la Creu – Cape Santa Pola Lighthouse loop from Santa Pola, a moderate 7.7 miles (12.4 km) path. This trail features coastal views and passes the prominent lighthouse. Local hikers also love the Santa Pola Promenade – Las Cadenas loop from Santa Pola, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through coastal paths and promenades, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Santa Pola is defined by fossil reef mountain ranges, flat salt marshes, and coastal paths. The network offers options for various fitness levels, from easy strolls to more challenging routes. The routes in Santa Pola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Pola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Santa Pola?
A: Santa Pola offers a diverse range of landscapes for hikers. You can explore the fossil reef mountain range of the Sierra de Santa Pola, extensive flat salt marshes in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7113457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Pola Salt Flats Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, and scenic coastal paths with views of the Mediterranean. Areas like Barranc de Salt also provide wilder ravine environments.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Santa Pola?
A: Yes, Santa Pola has over 50 easy hiking routes. For a gentle coastal walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19393339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Pola Promenade – Las Cadenas loop from Santa Pola&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 7.9 km long and takes around 2 hours. The flat terrains of the Santa Pola Salt Marshes also offer easy, long walks suitable for all ages.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Santa Pola?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Santa Pola are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868999946/sierra-de-santa-pola-circular-pr-cv-61" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Santa Pola (Circular PR-CV 61)&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of Santa Pola and Alicante.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints to see on the trails?
A: Hikers can enjoy several natural attractions and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Anguilla Observatory&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4036358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre El Rincón Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt; in the salt marshes are excellent for birdwatching, especially flamingos. The Cape Santa Pola Lighthouse area also offers spectacular coastal views, considered among the most beautiful in the Valencian Community.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Santa Pola dog-friendly?
A: Many of Santa Pola's natural areas and trails are suitable for dogs, especially the coastal paths and the extensive salt marshes. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and respect local regulations, particularly in protected areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7113457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Pola Salt Flats Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, to protect wildlife.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Pola?
A: Santa Pola's mild Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for hiking year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer treks. Even in summer, coastal walks can be enjoyable, especially during cooler mornings or evenings. Winter provides crisp, clear days perfect for exploring without the heat.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical sites or unique landmarks?
A: Yes, some trails incorporate historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868999946/sierra-de-santa-pola-circular-pr-cv-61" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Santa Pola (Circular PR-CV 61)&lt;/a&gt; passes by remnants from the Civil War. The Salt Museum, housed in an old salt factory within the Santa Pola Salt Marshes, offers insights into the region's salt extraction history and can be visited during a hike through the park.

Q: How long do hikes in Santa Pola typically take?
A: Hiking durations vary significantly depending on the route. Shorter coastal strolls, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19393339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Pola Promenade – Las Cadenas loop&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in about 2 hours. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e869012004/santa-pola-municipal-loop-trail-stage-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Pola Municipal Loop Trail: Stage 2&lt;/a&gt; through the salt marshes, can take over 7 hours.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Santa Pola?
A: The hiking routes in Santa Pola are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse scenery, from coastal views to salt marshes, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Santa Pola?
A: For more experienced hikers, Santa Pola offers several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e869012004/santa-pola-municipal-loop-trail-stage-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Pola Municipal Loop Trail: Stage 2&lt;/a&gt;, at over 28 km through the salt marshes, is considered difficult. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27639443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape Santa Pola Lighthouse – Steep Ascent with Coastal View loop&lt;/a&gt;, which includes significant elevation changes and stunning coastal vistas.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching along the hiking trails?
A: Santa Pola is an excellent destination for birdwatching, particularly in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7113457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Pola Salt Flats Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. This protected area is home to flamingos and various other waterfowl. Trails through the salt marshes often lead to observation huts and viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Anguilla Observatory&lt;/a&gt;, providing great opportunities to spot local birdlife.

Q: Can I access hiking trails in Santa Pola using public transport?
A: While specific public transport routes directly to all trailheads might vary, Santa Pola itself is well-connected. Many trails, especially those starting from the town center or along the coast, are accessible by local bus services or are within walking distance from bus stops. For routes further afield, a short taxi ride might be necessary.
tips: This beach stands out for its rugged character as it remains virgin and can only be accessed on foot. Together with the landscape of the salt flats behind, they make a combo worth visiting and enjoying.
From the tower you can see wonderful views of both the Salines Natural Park and El Pinet beach. A recommended site for the whole year!

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Santa Pola Municipal Loop Trail: Stage 2?
faqs.answer: While the trail is largely flat with minimal elevation gain (around 36 meters), it is classified as difficult primarily due to its considerable length of approximately 28.3 kilometers. The main challenge lies in the distance and the potential for humid or wet conditions on some paths, especially near the salt flats. Hikers should be prepared for a long day on their feet.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this stage of the trail?
faqs.answer: This stage predominantly navigates the flat, unique landscape of the salt marsh area, including the Salinas de Bras del Port and the Natural Park of the Salt Flats of Santa Pola. You'll encounter long, straight stretches, and paths that may be damp or slightly flooded depending on the season. A notable feature is the potential crossing of the Río Vinolopo, which might require removing shoes. The Pinet Beach area also offers a different coastal feel.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or natural features along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a close encounter with the unique ecosystem of the Santa Pola salt flats. Key features include the Dolores Trail, water entry ponds to the salt flats, and the 'magical and natural environment' of the Camí de la Costera de Boix. You can also visit Pinet Beach and the El Pinet Observation Tower. The area is renowned for its rich birdlife, including flamingos, which can be observed in the salt lakes and freshwater ponds.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is generally during the cooler months, from autumn through spring, to avoid the intense summer heat. Starting early in the day is recommended year-round to avoid peak sun hours and to allow ample time to complete the long distance. Be aware that paths can be damp or wet depending on recent rainfall.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Santa Pola Municipal Loop Trail: Stage 2?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations for dogs within the Parc Natural de les Salines de Santa Pola should be checked. Generally, dogs are often permitted on trails in natural parks but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Parc Natural de les Salines de Santa Pola?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to hike the trails within the Parc Natural de les Salines de Santa Pola. However, visitors are expected to respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and adhere to any posted regulations regarding wildlife protection or specific areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park and what is the trailhead access for this stage?
faqs.answer: As Stage 2 is part of a larger loop, there isn't one single designated 'trailhead' for this specific stage. However, common access points would be near Santa Pola or the Pinet side of the Natural Park. For specific parking, look for public parking areas in Santa Pola or near the Pinet Beach area, which offers convenient access to the salt flats section of the trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and potential conditions, it's crucial to bring sufficient water, especially during warmer months. Appropriate footwear that can handle potentially damp or wet paths is essential. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also highly recommended due to the exposed, flat terrain. Snacks or a packed lunch are advisable for the long duration.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Santa Pola Municipal Loop Trail: Stage 2 is primarily part of the Vuelta al término de Santa Pola (Etapa 2) route. It also intersects with significant portions of El Camí IT5TR3 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, and briefly with the Vuelta al término de Santa Pola (Etapa 1) and the Camí de la Costera de Boix.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities available along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily passes through natural and salt marsh areas, so amenities like cafes or shops are not directly on the route for much of its length. It's best to stock up on supplies in Santa Pola before starting your hike. You might find some establishments closer to the Pinet Beach area or at the start/end points in Santa Pola itself.
